message: Stage -1.2 drafted angle=0 v1 mode=cold-start runner=codex/gpt-5.5 status=completed

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b (Unsalvageable conjecture, Stage 0.5 reject fast-path) — boundary is stage_0.5_to_0, latest status is reject, and reviewer grounds are conjecture-level: the headline Theorem 1 is a definition-body restatement (eta_w^*(P) is defined as the first radius at which support faces intersect, making the iff tautological) AND the locked-parameter block declares a PartialID support-function anchor while run metadata declares exactid. Regression pattern (a): both rounds flag the same definition-body tautology and same anchor-drift; another Stage 0 round on the same .tex would burn budget against an unsalvageable conjecture.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture at stage_0.5_to_0 reject) — reviewer marks novelty as reject with tier_genuinely_below flagship floor; the main theorem is identified as a generic compactness/support-function fact and the two-shock formula its 1D specialization, neither a new named-estimator frontier nor a diagnose-then-correct result. This is conjecture-level, not a fixable derivation slip; another Stage 0 pass on the same .tex would still face the same novelty-floor verdict. Locked-parameter drift (PartialID vs exactid) compounds but is secondary.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b — boundary stage_0.5_to_0 with status reject on conjecture-level grounds: cluster mismatch (exactid declared but locked-parameter block at lines 190-200 anchors a PartialID/support-function problem, explicitly acknowledged at lines 550-551), and the headline Theorem critical-leakage-frontier (lines 302-305, proof 370-402) is a definitional unfold of eta_w^*(P) (lines 267-270) rather than a flagship result. Reviewer's tier verdict is tier_genuinely_below. These are not localized derivation slips a Stage 0 re-derive can fix — the conjecture as stated either belongs to a different cluster or collapses to its own definition. Re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget.

Review boundary stage_0.5_to_0 routed to stage_neg1 (reject fast-path): Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture, stage_0.5 reject fast-path): reviewer's novelty verdict is `reject` with `proposal_promise_gap: tier_genuinely_below` — the headline equivalence `h_LP = h_eta iff eta >= eta_w*(P)` is a near-immediate support-function unfold of the very definition of `eta_w*(P)` (first radius at which the LP maximizer set meets `Q_eta(P)`), so the kernel is definitionally tautological rather than a flagship equivalence frontier between named LP and non-Gaussian SVAR estimators. Compounded by cluster anchor drift (locked block declares PartialID support-function anchor while run metadata + Lean target are `exactid`), the .tex is not salvageable as the same conjecture — re-deriving on the existing statement would burn Stage 0 budget on a sub-flagship kernel. Not a derivation slip (would be `stage_0`); not a localized typo (would be `stage_4d`); not assumption strengthening (no Bucket A signal).
